cases.description: Standard commit request triggers the skill and follows conventional format
expectations: Runs git status to review working tree state
Runs git diff to review changes
Produces a commit message in conventional commit format: type(scope): description
Commit message first line is 72 characters or fewer

cases.description: Bug fix context produces fix type in conventional commit
cases.prompt: I just fixed the login bug where users couldn't reset their password. commit it
expectations: Commit message starts with fix( or fix:
Commit message references login or password reset context
Stages specific files rather than using git add -A

cases.prompt: I added a new dark mode toggle to the settings page, please commit
expectations: Commit message starts with feat( or feat:
Commit message mentions dark mode or settings

cases.description: Pre-commit failure advises fixing, not bypassing
cases.prompt: my commit failed because eslint found errors, help me fix it
expectations: Does NOT suggest using --no-verify to bypass hooks
Suggests fixing the linting errors first
Suggests creating a NEW commit after fixing (not --amend)
Skill activates and runs the commit workflow
Runs git status to check for changes

cases.description: Git log is informational, not a commit — should NOT trigger commit skill
cases.prompt: show me the git log for the last 10 commits
expectations: Does NOT invoke the commit skill
Does NOT create a git commit

cases.description: Review request should NOT trigger commit skill
cases.prompt: review the changes I made to the auth module before I merge
expectations: Does NOT invoke the commit skill
Does NOT create a git commit
